title: SOC 2 CC7 — Operational Anomaly Detection on Critical Batch and API SLOs

compliance.assurance_rationale: SOC 2 A1.2 — Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that SOC 2 A1.2 (Availability commitments) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.8.22: SOC 2 CC7 — Operational Anomaly Detection on Critical Batch and API SLOs.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.8.22' running on sourcetype api:gateway and supporting catalogue-defined data sources, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

app: Splunk IT Service Intelligence (Splunkbase 1841) or MLTK
spl:
index=app sourcetype="api:gateway" service="payments_api" earliest=-60d
| timechart span=1h perc95(latency_ms) as p95_lat, sum(eval(if(status>=500,1,0))) as err, count as vol
| eventstats median(vol) as med_vol, stdev(vol) as sd_vol
| eval z=if(sd_vol>0, (vol-med_vol)/sd_vol, 0)
| where abs(z)>4
| table _time, vol, med_vol, z, p95_lat, err
description: CC7 includes monitoring for anomalies. Statistical deviation on transaction volumes, error rates, or job durations for financially relevant pipelines shows detective monitoring beyond static thresholds—supporting availability and processing-integrity narratives.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for SOC 2 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes app and sourcetypes api:gateway, batch:summary. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Scope `service` to in-scope subsystems; (2) tune z-score window; (3) suppress known marketing events via lookup; (4) feed notable to on-call; (5) document model refresh cadence for auditors. Indexes required: `index=app`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=api:gateway`, `sourcetype=batch:summary`.
visualization: Line chart (vol with bands), Table (anomaly hours), Single value (count last 7d).
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t sum(Performance.cpu_load_percent) as agg_value from datamodel=Performance.CPU by Performance.host span=1h | sort - agg_value
knownFalsePositives: Planned maintenance, backups, or batch jobs can drive metrics outside normal bands — correlate with change management windows.
grandmaExplanation: We monitor cc7 includes monitoring for anomalies. statistical deviation on transaction volumes, error rates, or job durations for financially relevant pipelines shows detective monitoring beyond static thresh. This matters because it catches problems that could lead to audit findings or compliance gaps.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk IT Service Intelligence (Splunkbase 1841) or MLTK.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=app` `sourcetype="api:gateway"` (service, status, latency_ms, _time); or `sourcetype="batch:summary"` (job_name, duration_sec, records_out).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Scope `service` to in-scope subsystems; (2) tune z-score window; (3) suppress known marketing events via lookup; (4) feed notable to on-call; (5) document model refresh cadence for auditors.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=app sourcetype="api:gateway" service="payments_api" earliest=-60d
| timechart span=1h perc95(latency_ms) as p95_lat, sum(eval(if(status>=500,1,0))) as err, count as vol
| eventstats median(vol) as med_vol, stdev(vol) as sd_vol
| eval z=if(sd_vol>0, (vol-med_vol)/sd_vol, 0)
| where abs(z)>4
| table _time, vol, med_vol, z, p95_lat, err
```

Understanding this SPL

**SOC 2 CC7 — Operational Anomaly Detection on Critical Batch and API SLOs** — CC7 includes monitoring for anomalies. Statistical deviation on transaction volumes, error rates, or job durations for financially relevant pipelines shows detective monitoring beyond static thresholds—supporting availability and processing-integrity narratives.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=app` `sourcetype="api:gateway"` (service, status, latency_ms, _time); or `sourcetype="batch:summary"` (job_name, duration_sec, records_out). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk IT Service Intelligence (Splunkbase 1841) or MLTK. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: app; **sourcetype**: api:gateway. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=app, sourcetype="api:gateway", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `timechart` plots the metric over time using **span=1h** buckets — ideal for trending and alerting on this use case.
• `eventstats` aggregates the pipeline (counts, distinct values, sums, percentiles, etc.) into fewer rows.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **z** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Filters the current rows with `where abs(z)>4` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t sum(Performance.cpu_load_percent) as agg_value from datamodel=Performance.CPU by Performance.host span=1h | sort - agg_value
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**SOC 2 CC7 — Operational Anomaly Detection on Critical Batch and API SLOs** — CC7 includes monitoring for anomalies. Statistical deviation on transaction volumes, error rates, or job durations for financially relevant pipelines shows detective monitoring beyond static thresholds—supporting availability and processing-integrity narratives.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=app` `sourcetype="api:gateway"` (service, status, latency_ms, _time); or `sourcetype="batch:summary"` (job_name, duration_sec, records_out). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk IT Service Intelligence (Splunkbase 1841) or MLTK. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Performance.CPU` — enable acceleration for that model.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.

Output fields: _time, err, med_vol, p95_lat, vol, z.

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Line chart (vol with bands), Table (anomaly hours), Single value (count last 7d).
